tool_args.label: Transit Wireless License Expansion - Cellular and Wi-Fi
tool_args.raw_text: Transit Wireless license for cellular in the NYCT tunnels and Wi-Fi at above ground NYCT stations and Staten Island Railway

payload.project_name: Transit Wireless License Expansion for Cellular in NYCT Tunnels and Wi-Fi at Above Ground NYCT Stations and Staten Island Railway
payload.description: Expansion of Transit Wireless license to design, build, operate, maintain and sublicense a wireless communication connection system in underground tunnels, at above-ground stations and along the ROW, a free public and private facing Wi-Fi network for NYCT above-ground and SIR stations, and the non-exclusive right to market fiber optic cable to private customers. TW estimates capex investment in excess of $600 million. The project results in over $1 Billion in benefit for MTA and its customers over the life of the agreement.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Infrastructure build is at no cost to the MTA with TW responsible for all capital expenses. TW estimates the capex investment in excess of $600 million.

Approval of commercial terms for TW's to exclusively design, build, operate, maintain and sublicense (i) a wireless communication connection system in underground tunnels, at above-ground stations and along the ROW to improve cell carriers' local signals, (ii) a free public and private facing Wi-Fi network for NYCT above-ground and SIR stations
The Project results in over $1 Billion in benefit for the MTA and its customers over the life of the agreement.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block
evidence_refs[1].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: First/Last Mile License Policy for Suburban Stations
tool_args.raw_text: Adoption of board policy to license property in support of First/Last Mile improvements at suburban commuter rail stations

payload.project_name: First/Last Mile License Policy for Suburban Commuter Rail Stations
payload.description: Policy to permit MTA Real Estate, in partnership with MTA Transit Oriented Development (TOD), to enter into license agreements with suburban counties, municipalities, non-profit organizations, or private entities to provide, manage and maintain first/last mile amenities at MTA suburban passenger stations including pedestrian improvements, bicycle/scooter access improvements, parking for shared ride opportunities, and shared microtransit.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Approval of a policy to permit MTA Real Estate, in partnership with MTA Transit Oriented Development ('TOD'), to enter into licenses with suburban counties, municipalities, non-profit organizations, or private entities to provide first/last mile amenities at MTA suburban passenger stations

Pedestrian improvements (e.g., marked crosswalks, additional sidewalks) Bicycle/scooter access improvements (e.g., bike parking, bike lanes, bike sharing, scooter sharing, moped sharing, e-bike, e-scooter, moped) Parking for shared ride opportunities (e.g., carpooling, vanpooling, shuttle, Uber, Lyft, Via) Shared microtransit including on-demand transit services

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block
evidence_refs[1].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Purchase of 5 Nova Bus All-Electric Buses
tool_args.raw_text: Furnish and Deliver 5 Low-Floor 40-foot All-Electric Buses from Nova Bus (US) Inc.

payload.project_name: Purchase of 5 Low-Floor 40-foot All-Electric Buses (AEBs) from Nova Bus
payload.description: Purchase of five low-floor 40-foot all-electric buses from Nova Bus (US) Inc. as part of MTA's long-range plan to transition to a completely zero-emissions bus fleet by year 2040. Buses will operate between the Bronx and upper Manhattan and will utilize in-depot charging stations at Kingsbridge Depot.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC Transit requests that the Board waive formal competitive bidding... and approve the purchase of five low-floor 40-foot all-electric buses ('AEB') as well as related items such as spare parts, special tools and equipment, technical documentation, and training from Nova Bus (US) Inc. ('Nova') in the total estimated amount of $8,275,562.
This procurement is part of a key initiative by the Department of Buses as the MTA develops a long-range plan to transition to a completely zero-emissions bus fleet by year 2040.
The five low-floor 40-foot AEBs in this contract will operate between the Bronx and upper Manhattan and will utilize the in-depot charging stations already installed at Kingsbridge Depot.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: HASTUS Scheduling System Upgrade
tool_args.raw_text: Upgrade of HASTUS Scheduling System - contract with Giro Inc. for $7,922,713

payload.project_name: HASTUS Scheduling System Upgrade
payload.description: Six-year, noncompetitive miscellaneous service contract to Giro Inc. to upgrade the existing HASTUS Scheduling System for MTA Bus and NYC Transit. Upgrade to HASTUS 2022 version includes merging MTABC and DOB installations, integrating new MinBus module for electric bus operations, and creating separate HASTUS Rail 2022 application for subway scheduling. Total cost $7,922,713 with a 50 percent reduction from Giro's standard upgrade license fees.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Giro, Inc. $7,922,713 ... Award of a six-year miscellaneous service contract to Giro Inc. to upgrade the existing HASTUS Scheduling System for MTA Bus and NYC Transit.
MTA Headquarters seeks approval to award a six-year, noncompetitive miscellaneous service contract to Giro Inc. to upgrade the existing HASTUS Scheduling System in the amount of $7,922,713.

HASTUS version 2022 is a significantly improved and more powerful product compared to the current versions, specifically with respect to bus network management, data interface, development capabilities and scheduling.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.raw_text: Transit Wireless license for cellular in the NYCT tunnels and Wi-Fi at above ground NYCT stations and Staten Island Railway

payload.project_name: Transit Wireless License Expansion for Cellular in NYCT Tunnels and Wi-Fi at Above Ground NYCT Stations and Staten Island Railway
payload.description: Expansion of Transit Wireless license to design, build, operate, maintain and sublicense a wireless communication connection system in underground tunnels, at above-ground stations and along the ROW, a free public and private facing Wi-Fi network for NYCT above-ground and SIR stations, and the non-exclusive right to market fiber optic cable. TW estimates capex investment in excess of $600 million. Results in over $1 Billion in benefit for MTA.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Approval of commercial terms for TW's to exclusively design, build, operate, maintain and sublicense (i) a wireless communication connection system in underground tunnels, at above-ground stations and along the ROW to improve cell carriers' local signals, (ii) a free public and private facing Wi-Fi network for NYCT above-ground and SIR stations; (iii) and the non-exclusive right to market fiber optic cable to private customers (collectively the "Project").
FRANCHISEE: Transit Wireless, LLC ("TW")
The Project results in over $1 Billion in benefit for the MTA and its customers over the life of the agreement.

tool_args.raw_text: Furnish and Deliver 5 Low-Floor 40-foot All-Electric Buses from Nova Bus (US) Inc.

payload.project_name: Purchase of 5 Low-Floor 40-foot All-Electric Buses (AEBs) from Nova Bus
payload.description: Purchase of five low-floor 40-foot all-electric buses from Nova Bus (US) Inc. as part of MTA's long-range plan to transition to a completely zero-emissions bus fleet by year 2040. Buses will operate between the Bronx and upper Manhattan and will utilize in-depot charging stations at Kingsbridge Depot.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC Transit requests that the Board waive formal competitive bidding pursuant to Public Authorities Law, Section 1209, subdivision 9(d) to test a new source, product, and technology; evaluate the service and reliability of such product; and approve the purchase of five low-floor 40-foot all-electric buses ("AEB") as well as related items such as spare parts, special tools and equipment, technical documentation, and training from Nova Bus (US) Inc. ("Nova") in the total estimated amount of $8,275,562.
This procurement is part of a key initiative by the Department of Buses as the MTA develops a long-range plan to transition to a completely zero-emissions bus fleet by year 2040.
The five low-floor 40-foot AEBs in this contract will operate between the Bronx and upper Manhattan and will utilize the in-depot charging stations already installed at Kingsbridge Depot.

tool_args.raw_text: Upgrade of HASTUS Scheduling System - contract with Giro Inc. for $7,922,713

payload.project_name: HASTUS Scheduling System Upgrade
payload.description: Six-year noncompetitive miscellaneous service contract to Giro Inc. to upgrade the existing HASTUS Scheduling System for MTA Bus and NYC Transit. Upgrading to HASTUS 2022 will help eliminate inefficiencies in data and schedule development tasks.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 1. Giro, Inc. $7,922,713 Staff Summary Attached Six years Contract # 900000000004134 Award of a six-year miscellaneous service contract to Giro Inc. to upgrade the existing HASTUS Scheduling System for MTA Bus and NYC Transit.
MTA Headquarters seeks approval to award a six-year, noncompetitive miscellaneous service contract to Giro Inc. to upgrade the existing HASTUS Scheduling System in the amount of $7,922,713.

HASTUS version 2022 is a significantly improved and more powerful product compared to the current versions, specifically with respect to bus network management, data interface, development capabilities and scheduling.

tool_args.label: CBDTP Environmental Documentation - WSP
tool_args.raw_text: WSP USA Inc. contract modification for transportation planning services - CBDTP environmental documentation

payload.project_name: Central Business District Tolling Program Environmental Review
payload.description: Additional funding of $8.725 million for WSP USA to complete the environmental documentation for the Central Business District Tolling Program Environmental review, as required by the Federal Highway Administration under the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA). Includes finalizing the Environmental Assessment, preparing outreach materials, and developing final documentation.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Headquarters is seeking Board approval to increase the aggregate amount of the Transportation Planning and Research contract by the not-to-exceed amount of $8.725 million.
Additional funding is needed to complete the environmental documentation for CBDTP, as required by the Federal Highway Administration under the National Environmental Policy Act ("NEPA").

tool_args.label: TBTA Sales Tax Revenue Bonds Series 2022A
tool_args.raw_text: On July 20, 2022, MTA issued $700.000 million of Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority Sales Tax Revenue Bonds, Series 2022A

payload.description: Inaugural issuance of $700 million TBTA Sales Tax Revenue Bonds, Series 2022A (TBTA Capital Lockbox - City Sales Tax) on July 20, 2022. All-in TIC of 4.378%, final maturity May 15, 2062.

evidence_refs.source_quote: $700,000,000 Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority

On July 20, 2022, MTA issued $700.000 million of Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority Sales Tax Revenue Bonds, Series 2022A (TBTA Capital Lockbox - City Sales Tax). This was the inaugural issuance for this credit.

tool_args.label: NYCT Non-Reimbursable Overtime YTD June 2022
tool_args.raw_text: NYCT: $365M spent YTD and $120M unfavorable, primarily due to higher vacancy/absentee coverage, and severe weather response.

payload.description: NYCT non-reimbursable overtime: $365 million spent YTD, $120 million unfavorable vs budget, due to vacancy/absentee coverage and severe weather response.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYCT: $365M spent YTD and $120M unfavorable, primarily due to higher vacancy/absentee coverage, and severe weather response.

tool_args.label: GCT Net Revenue 2021
tool_args.raw_text: 2021 Net Revenue of $11.7M was only about 30% of 2019 pre-Covid figures.

payload.description: Grand Central Terminal net revenue for 2021 was $11.7 million, about 30% of 2019 pre-Covid figures.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In review of the Property Performance trends (see following page), 2021 Net Revenue of $11.7M was only about 30% of 2019 pre-Covid figures.
